figures.caption: Table 2 Forecasting results (MSE/MAE) on four ETT benchmarks (look-back T=336, standard chronological splits). Results for MSMixer, DLinear, and NLinear are averaged over 3 seeds. †denotes results from published papers. Bold: best MSE per row; underline: second best.
Fig. 5 Training and validation loss convergence for MSMixer on ETTh1 across all four prediction horizons. Early stopping halts training within 4–8 epochs for most configurations.

Fig. 1 Architecture of MSMixer. After RevIN normalisation, the channel-independent time series is downsampled at multiple temporal resolutions. Each resolution is independently processed by a lightweight MLP block. The multi-resolution outputs are merged via a learnable softmax gate and fused with a DLinear shortcut that provides global trend and seasonality context from the full lookback window.
Fig. 3 Ablation study results (ETTh1, H = 96). MSE comparison across architectural variants with error bars (±1 std).
Table 1 Parameter count breakdown of MSMixer (T = 336, H = 96, d = 64, S = {1, 4, 16}, N = 7).
